Hence it was called Ramatha. S. Levi places it between Gazni and

Division of Globe XIV 177
The Pnratas are identified with Ptolemy's Pardene in the
centre of Gedrosia (Baluchistan).
Kanaka is identified by Dey with Travancore. Kern takes
this word as an epithet of ^aka and translates the compound as
Gold-Scythians.
kakas are located in the south-west of India, in the Agnipurana
(LV. 16). Apart from the Scythian rulers of north-western India
Saka K§atrapas and Mahaksatrapas of the lineage of Bhtimaka and
Gastana are known to have ruled over western India in the early
centuries of the Christian era. In the Allahabad inscription they
are said to have paid homage to Samudragupta.
Mlecchas, according to Kern, are foreigners or barbarians. In
II. 15 supra the Greeks are spoken of as Mlecchas. In Alberuni's
time the term was used to denote the Arabs.
